items.statement: Localized adenopathy implies involvement of a single anatomic area.
items.explanation: Localized or regional adenopathy indicates involvement of a single anatomic area.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen | LYMPHADENOPATHY | Lymphadenopathy | CLINICAL ASSESSMENT

items.statement: Nodes <1.0 cm² in area are almost always secondary to benign, nonspecific reactive causes.
items.explanation: The passage states that nodes <1.0 cm² are almost always due to benign causes.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · 1. Infectious diseases (part 1)

items.statement: Tenderness in lymph nodes is usually secondary to malignant diseases.
items.explanation: Tenderness is typically found when the capsule is stretched during rapid enlargement, usually due to an inflammatory process.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · 1. Infectious diseases (part 1)

items.statement: Fine-needle aspiration should be the first diagnostic procedure for lymphadenopathy.
items.explanation: Fine-needle aspiration should not be performed as the first diagnostic procedure because most diagnoses require more tissue than such aspiration can provide.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · 1. Infectious diseases (part 2) | LABORATORY INVESTIGATION (part 1)

items.statement: <5% of lymphadenopathy patients in primary care practices will require a biopsy.
items.explanation: In primary care practices, <5% of lymphadenopathy patients will require a biopsy.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · 1. Infectious diseases (part 2) | LABORATORY INVESTIGATION (part 1)

items.subtopic: LABORATORY INVESTIGATION (part 2) | STRUCTURE AND FUNCTION OF THE SPLEEN
items.statement: The spleen is capable of extramedullary hematopoiesis in humans.
items.explanation: The spleen can generate cellular components of the blood when the marrow cannot meet the needs.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · LABORATORY INVESTIGATION (part 2) | STRUCTURE AND FUNCTION OF THE SPLEEN

items.subtopic: LABORATORY INVESTIGATION (part 2) | STRUCTURE AND FUNCTION OF THE SPLEEN
items.statement: Accessory spleens develop in all individuals.
items.explanation: Accessory spleens may develop in around 20% of persons when hillocks fail to unify into a single tissue mass.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · LABORATORY INVESTIGATION (part 2) | STRUCTURE AND FUNCTION OF THE SPLEEN

items.statement: A palpable spleen always indicates the presence of disease.
items.explanation: A palpable spleen does not always equate with the presence of disease, as it can be found in asymptomatic individuals.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · CLINICAL ASSESSMENT (part 1)

items.statement: The rupture of a spleen is often accompanied by pain.
items.explanation: Rupture of the spleen may be painless, despite potentially leading to serious complications.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · CLINICAL ASSESSMENT (part 1)

items.statement: Percussion is more reproducible among examiners than palpation.
items.explanation: Reproducibility among examiners is better for palpation than percussion.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · CLINICAL ASSESSMENT (part 2) | DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS

items.statement: MRI offers significant advantages over CT in assessing spleen size.
items.explanation: MRI appears to offer no advantage over CT for determining spleen size.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · CLINICAL ASSESSMENT (part 2) | DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS

items.statement: In hypersplenism, granulocytopenia is always sustained after splenectomy.
items.explanation: Reversal of cytopenia, particularly granulocytopenia, is sometimes not sustained after splenectomy.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · LABORATORY ASSESSMENT

items.statement: The major laboratory abnormalities accompanying splenomegaly are determined by the underlying systemic illness.
items.explanation: Splenomegaly's laboratory findings depend on the underlying condition.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · LABORATORY ASSESSMENT

items.statement: Splenectomy is necessary for staging in all stages of Hodgkin's lymphoma.
items.explanation: Splenectomy is only required for staging in clinical stage I or II Hodgkin's lymphoma when radiation therapy alone is considered.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · SPLENECTOMY (part 1)

items.statement: The presence of Howell-Jolly bodies post-splenectomy indicates normal splenic function.
items.explanation: Howell-Jolly bodies in the peripheral blood after splenectomy indicate loss of splenic culling and pitting function.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · SPLENECTOMY (part 1)

items.statement: Routine chemoprophylaxis with oral penicillin is recommended for splenectomized patients.
items.explanation: Routine chemoprophylaxis with oral penicillin can result in the emergence of drug-resistant strains and is not recommended.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · SPLENECTOMY (part 2)

items.statement: Splenectomized patients are more susceptible to viral infections.
items.explanation: No increased risk of viral infection has been noted in patients who have no spleen.
items.reference: Enlargement of Lymph Nodes and Spleen · SPLENECTOMY (part 2)

items.assertion: The majority of patients with lymphadenopathy have a nonspecific etiology.
items.reason: Most cases are due to viral or bacterial upper respiratory illnesses.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why most lymphadenopathy cases are nonspecific.

items.assertion: Generalized adenopathy is rarely associated with nonmalignant disorders.
items.reason: It is often linked to conditions like infectious mononucleosis or systemic lupus erythematosus.
items.explanation: The assertion is false because generalized adenopathy is frequently associated with nonmalignant disorders.

items.assertion: Mediastinal adenopathy in young patients is commonly associated with primary lung cancer.

items.explanation: The assertion is false; mediastinal adenopathy in young patients is not commonly associated with primary lung cancer.

items.assertion: A chest x-ray is usually positive in patients with lymphadenopathy.
items.reason: The presence of a pulmonary infiltrate or mediastinal lymphadenopathy would suggest serious conditions like tuberculosis or cancer.
items.explanation: The assertion is false because the chest x-ray is usually negative, but if positive, it suggests significant conditions.

items.assertion: The spleen's normal physiologic role includes the removal of antibody-coated bacteria from circulation.
items.reason: This function is part of its immune response capabilities.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains the assertion.

items.assertion: The spleen stores red blood cells for autotransfusion in humans.
items.reason: This function is observed in some animals under sympathetic stimuli.
items.explanation: The assertion is false because the normal human spleen does not store or contract red blood cells, while the reason is true for some animals.

items.assertion: Physical examination techniques for the spleen include palpation and percussion.
items.reason: These methods help in identifying splenic enlargement through various maneuvers like bimanual palpation and percussion of Traube's space.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why these techniques are used.

items.assertion: Splenomegaly always reflects the primary disease.
items.reason: In patients with Hodgkin's lymphoma, splenomegaly is due to direct involvement by the cancer in all cases.
items.explanation: The assertion is false because splenomegaly may not reflect the primary disease but rather a reaction to it.

items.assertion: Splenectomy can lead to significant tumor regression in certain leukemias.
items.reason: The spleen may produce hormones or growth factors affecting tumor cell proliferation.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, but the conjecture about hormone production is not substantiated.

items.assertion: Laparoscopic splenectomy results in longer hospital stays compared to open procedures.
